mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 5% indicates a tightening labor market, which signals stronger corporate earnings potential and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of instances where UNRATE falls below 5%, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns weakens to |rho| < 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust demand conditions, which drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through increased corporate revenue visibility.
failure_condition: The joint growth of GDPC1 and HOUST fails to produce positive 60d SPX returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 12-month moving average signals improved manufacturing activity, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through sectoral earnings upgrades.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 35% of instances where INDPRO exceeds its 12mo MA, or the signal's predictive power declines to Rank IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 3% triggers central bank hawkishness, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through rising bond yields and equity re-rating.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3%, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and SPX returns weakens to |rho| < 0.20.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real sales (RSAFS) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) indicates weakening consumer demand, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and UNRATE fails to produce negative 60d SPX returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Positive payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 100k signals strong employment momentum, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through sustained consumer spending.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 30% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k, or the signal's predictive power declines to Rank IC < 0.07.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 95 indicates recovering manufacturing activity, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through sectoral momentum.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 45% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 95, or the signal's predictive power declines to Rank IC < 0.04.
